channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'My fault to hire him': Spaniard's coach quits mid-French Open via text
item.description: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

media:group.media:description: Alejandro Davidovich Fokina had his coach abandon him prior to his second-round loss at the French Open.

item.title: Live: ABC experts take your questions about Iran war as it enters fourth month
item.description: ABC experts are taking questions abut the Iran war as the conflict enters its fourth month with no clear end in sight. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Georgie Hewson

media:group.media:description: Join us for our Q and A as ABC experts answer your questions about the Middle East war.

item.title: Esperance on cusp of expanding biofuel industry
item.description: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas R Ward

media:group.media:description: There is uncertainty about the future of fuel prices.

item.title: Slater refuses to guarantee Walsh recall despite Maroons' collapse
item.description: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

media:group.media:description: Billy Slater has refused to confirm an Origin recall for Reece Walsh despite his side's collapse.

item.title: Police charge five teens after stolen car rampage in Cairns
item.description: After chaotic scenes in Cairns yesterday morning, police say they have arrested five teenagers.
item.dc:creator: Holly Richardson

media:group.media:description: Police have released footage of three allegedly stolen cars in Cairns.

item.title: Breaking: Man charged with murder after several people attacked in regional Queensland
item.description: A 36-year-old man has been charged with murder after allegedly attacking several people south of Bundaberg.

media:group.media:description: The alleged attacks spanned multiple crime scenes.

item.title: Live: ASX set top open lower as price of oil drops
item.description: The Aussie share market is set to open lower as the price of oil drops. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Young investors who take bigger risks will pay more tax under the government's proposed changes to CGT.

item.title: WA teachers to be trained to better support children with higher needs
item.description: The WA government has committed $16 million for a professional learning hub that will include mandatory inductions for special needs education assistants and disability modules for graduate teachers.
item.dc:creator: Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: WA is the only state that allows a principal to deny enrolment to a student if a school does not have an "appropriate educational program".

item.title: Live: Budget's negative gearing, CGT legislation to be introduced to parliament
item.description: The federal government will introduce legislation to parliament today to overhaul negative gearing and the capital gains tax. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Albanese watches on as Jim Chalmers delivers the 2026 federal budget.

item.title: Legal action filed over decision to clear almost 3,000ha of tropical savanna
item.description: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Environment Minister Murray Watt says xxxx

item.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder
item.description: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: The man met his victim on the dating app Tinder before he indecently abused her in 2024.

item.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf
item.description: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.
item.dc:creator: Emma Pedler, Duncan Bailey, and Jenae Madden

media:group.media:description: Jochen Kaempf predicts the algal bloom will "hang around much longer" than first thought.

item.title: Why is Australia experiencing summer-like storms days out from winter?
item.description: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.
item.dc:creator: Tom Saunders

media:group.media:description: Parts of the east could experience one of its wettest May's ever recorded.

item.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous
item.description: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: Stolen Generations brothers Allen Edwards and Iain Seymour at Colebrook Reconciliation Park on May 12, 2026.

item.title: Teenager stabbed at Melbourne shopping centre as police release knife data
item.description: The fight broke out in the dining and entertainment precinct of Highpoint Shopping Centre in Maribyrnong.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Brown

media:group.media:description: Police are investigating a stabbing during a fight at Highpoint on Wednesday night.

item.title: Explosions heard as blaze destroys Brunswick bike shop
item.description: Firefighters responded to separate blazes in Melbourne overnight, with police investigating one as an arson attack.

media:group.media:description: A firefighter combats a blaze in Brunswick on Wednesday night.

item.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' on agricultural industry
item.description: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Wilkes

media:group.media:description: The Avoca-Bealiba Road bridge was built in 1942 and is one of many aging bridges that cannot hold B-doubles.

item.title: As the debate heats up, migrants are quietly keeping the bush running
item.description: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter, Emily Doak, and Anita Ward

media:group.media:description: PALM scheme workers play an important role in many industries based in regional Australia.

item.title: A pipe bursts every 73 minutes in Qld, report warns situation set to worsen
item.description: Queensland councils are calling for more state funding to update the state's pipe network, which they say has deteriorated to a high-risk condition.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Moon

item.title: Farmers desperate for nitrogen 'drug' search for substitutes
item.description: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.
item.dc:creator: Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: Marcus Burns is a third-generation apple grower at Spreyton in Tasmania's north-west.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
item.description: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

media:group.media:description: Matthew Perry's assistant played a key role in the star's ketamine addiction.

item.title: 'We need to talk about sex': Young people call for sex ed to grow up
item.description: A long-running survey of Australian teens has identified falling condom use and unwanted sex as major concerns for young people starting relationships.
item.dc:creator: Conor Duffy and Rhiannon Hobbins

media:group.media:description: One in five teens aged 14 to 18 that were surveyed, said they experienced unwanted sex.

item.title: 'How low will they stoop?' Apology demanded over leak to media
item.description: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Ruth Forrest (centre) wants to know why a "confidential internal email ended up in the hands of the media".

item.title: Labor's CGT challenge deepens as biotech sector raises alarm
item.description: Another sector has called for a capital gains tax change carve-out, warning the government that it will face a talent drain without it.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers says amending legislation after it has been introduced is "not unusual".

item.title: Torquay wagtails increasing in numbers around the world
item.description: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Jonathon Kendall

media:group.media:description: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.
